title: fragaria virginiana subsp. glauca
intro: Fragaria virginiana subsp. glauca, also known as the woodland strawberry or glaucous strawberry, is a subspecies of wild strawberry native to eastern North America. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high content of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Fragaria virginiana subsp. glauca have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and may also have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
